issue.title: Aside recommended articles hidden on Reach PLC sites

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

labels.name: A: Cannot reproduce
labels.description: Filters devs cannot reproduce this issue

issue.body:
I found this problem from https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/pull/230853#discussion_r3207728418 On article pages for four Reach PLC sites[^sites], the aside section contains both recommended articles and ad units. When AdGuard is active, **all article cards in the aside are hidden**, leaving an empty sidebar. [^sites]: UK news publisher: https://www.reachplc.com/about-us/our-brands ### Affected sites - www.birminghammail.co.uk - www.mirror.co.uk - www.liverpoolecho.co.uk - www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk ### Suspected rules The aside rules in `BaseFilter/sections/specific.txt` (around [lines 5419–5425](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/b671a94378b904207a3a9c52e576273b86a00ec0/BaseFilter/sections/specific.txt#L5419-L5425)) are likely hiding editorial content alongside ad containers: ```adblock birminghammail.co.uk,...##aside span > span.react-loading-skeleton birminghammail.co.uk,...##aside > div[class^="sc-"]:has(> span > span.react-loading-skeleton) birminghammail.co.uk,...##section[data-testid^="commercial-"] ``` - The `sc-` prefixed styled-components selector with `:has(react-loading-skeleton)` - may apply before content hydrates by React, permanently hiding article card containers. - The `[data-testid^="commercial-"]` prefix may also match editorial sections.
Screenshots without AdGuard
Screenshots with AdGuard active

issue.title: Aside recommended articles hidden on Reach PLC sites

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking

issue.body:
I found this problem from https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/pull/230853#discussion_r3207728418 On article pages for four Reach PLC sites[^sites], the aside section contains both recommended articles and ad units. When AdGuard is active, **all article cards in the aside are hidden**, leaving an empty sidebar. [^sites]: UK news publisher: https://www.reachplc.com/about-us/our-brands ### Affected sites - www.birminghammail.co.uk - www.mirror.co.uk - www.liverpoolecho.co.uk - www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk ### Suspected rules The aside rules in `BaseFilter/sections/specific.txt` (around [lines 5419–5425](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/b671a94378b904207a3a9c52e576273b86a00ec0/BaseFilter/sections/specific.txt#L5419-L5425)) are likely hiding editorial content alongside ad containers: ```adblock birminghammail.co.uk,...##aside span > span.react-loading-skeleton birminghammail.co.uk,...##aside > div[class^="sc-"]:has(> span > span.react-loading-skeleton) birminghammail.co.uk,...##section[data-testid^="commercial-"] ``` - The `sc-` prefixed styled-components selector with `:has(react-loading-skeleton)` - may apply before content hydrates by React, permanently hiding article card containers. - The `[data-testid^="commercial-"]` prefix may also match editorial sections.
Screenshots without AdGuard
Screenshots with AdGuard active
### Expected behavior Ad units in the aside are hidden. Recommended article cards remain visible. ### Notes Need a selector that distinguishes ad-only containers from mixed editorial+ad containers, or a more specific `data-testid` value.

comment.body: For now, close it.

### Issue URL (Ads) [https://rutube.ru/video/eda43d593fd1ecbadf9d63db6309f205/](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Frutube.ru%2Fvideo%2Feda43d593fd1ecbadf9d63db6309f205%2F) ### Comment > С недавнего времени реклама сайта Ru Tube начала появляться в плеере. comment.body:
Not reproduced on my end.

Please try following actions, then check the problem again:
1. Disable all content blockers in Safari settings and enable them again
2. Restart Safari
3. Restart AdGuard
4. Check for filters update in AdGuard.

### Important:
* The filters update process is fully completed ~10 seconds after the manual filter update check is completed.

### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.skt.tmap.ku](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplay.google.com%2Fstore%2Fapps%2Fdetails%3Fid%3Dcom.skt.tmap.ku) ### Comment > > 안녕하세요. > 최근 TMAP 사용 중 검색 화면에서 간헐적으로 서비스 오류가 발생하고 있습니다. > > 증상은 다음과 같습니다. > > 검색창(장소/주소 검색) 진입 시 > “일시적으로 서비스를 이용할 수 없습니다. 잠시 후 다시 접속해 주세요.” > 메시지가 출력되며 검색 기능이 동작하지 않음 > > 와이파이, 데이터 네트워크 환경에서 반복적으로 발생 > > 광고 차단 앱(AdGuard) 사용 중 발생 > > AdGuard 필터 또는 DNS 차단과 관련된 충돌 가능성이 있어 보임 > ### Screenshots

issue.title: Google Play: com.skt.tmap.ku

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

issue.body:
### Issue URL (Incorrect Blocking) [https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.skt.tmap.ku](https://adguardteam.github.io/AnonymousRedirect/redirect.html?url=https%3A%2F%2Fplay.google.com%2Fstore%2Fapps%2Fdetails%3Fid%3Dcom.skt.tmap.ku) ### Comment > > 안녕하세요. > 최근 TMAP 사용 중 검색 화면에서 간헐적으로 서비스 오류가 발생하고 있습니다. > > 증상은 다음과 같습니다. > > 검색창(장소/주소 검색) 진입 시 > “일시적으로 서비스를 이용할 수 없습니다. 잠시 후 다시 접속해 주세요.” > 메시지가 출력되며 검색 기능이 동작하지 않음 > > 와이파이, 데이터 네트워크 환경에서 반복적으로 발생 > > 광고 차단 앱(AdGuard) 사용 중 발생 > > AdGuard 필터 또는 DNS 차단과 관련된 충돌 가능성이 있어 보임 > ### Screenshots

comment.body:
This issue has been automatically marked as stale because it has been open for 3 days with no activity.
Unfortunately, our resources are limited and we can not always take the time to respond to requests about websites that are not popular enough. The issue will be closed if no further activity occurs.
Please note, that AdGuard Filters is an open-source project that is used by different content blockers and we welcome contributions. If you have some technical knowledge about how websites work, you are welcome to create a pull request following [these instructions](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md).

comment.body:
> The json-prune rule targets promoted/sponsored ad feed items that contain promo/ad tracking metadata

I mean does it remove such posts from the feed?

> The “Local publisher” cards are not the same as the normal sponsored ad payload.

You can try this rule, but the saved page will help to see the actual HTML code. Please send it via the website, not email.
```adblock
nextdoor.com#?#div[data-v3-view-type="V3Wrapper"]:has(> span[data-testid="feed-item-visibility-tracker"] > div[id^="feedItem_"] span[class*="Item"]:contains(/Sponsored|Local publisher/i))
```

comment.body:
> On May 15, 2026, at 6:55 AM, zloyden ***@***.***> wrote:
> 
> 
> zloyden
>  left a comment 
> (AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters#231311)
>  <https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/231311#issuecomment-4459159757>
> @svan71 <https://github.com/svan71> Hi, I don't have an account to check this. Are all rules necessary? As I see, the json-prune one should be enough. Is it work?
> We also have a similar rule to this nextdoor.com#?#div[data-v3-view-type="V3Wrapper"]:has(> [data-testid="feed-item-visibility-tracker"]:contains(/Sponsored/i)). Could you send a saved page in zip archive here?
> 
> —
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub <https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/issues/231311#issuecomment-4459159757>, or unsubscribe <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/ALU3J3U4QPT2AZEOW4W7RT3423ZSVAVCNFSM6AAAAACY7HVVPSVHI2DSMVQWIX3LMV43OSLTON2WKQ3PNVWWK3TUHM2DINJZGE2TSNZVG4>.
> Triage notifications on the go with GitHub Mobile for iOS <https://apps.apple.com/app/apple-store/id1477376905?ct=notification-email&mt=8&pt=524675> or Android <https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.github.android&referrer=utm_campaign%3Dnotification-email%26utm_medium%3Demail%26utm_source%3Dgithub>. 
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> 

> They are two different cases.
> 

1. The json-prune rule targets promoted/sponsored ad feed items that contain promo/ad tracking metadata:

nextdoor.com#%#//scriptlet('json-prune', 'data.me.personalizedFeed.feedItems.[-].promo.creative.clickThroughUrl.adsTrackingMetadata.metadata.adRequestId')

2. The V3Wrapper cosmetic rule targets feed cards visibly labeled “Local publisher” / “Sponsored” after they are already rendered:

nextdoor.com#?#div[data-v3-view-type="V3Wrapper"]:has(> [data-testid="feed-item-visibility-tracker"]:contains(/Local publisher/i))

The “Local publisher” cards are not the same as the normal sponsored ad payload. They are local/publisher/promotional posts that still appear even when normal ad scripts and telemetry are blocked.

There is already a similar Sponsored rule, but I needed the Local publisher variant too.

comment.body:
@svan71 Hi, I don't have an account to check this. Are all rules necessary? As I see, the `json-prune` one should be enough. Does it work?
We also have a similar rule to this `nextdoor.com#?#div[data-v3-view-type="V3Wrapper"]:has(> [data-testid="feed-item-visibility-tracker"]:contains(/Sponsored/i))`. Could you send a saved page in zip archive here?
